get; set; }\n        public string Model { get; set; }\n        public string Brand { get; set; }\n        public CarType Type { get; set; }\n        public bool IsAvailable { get; set; }\n        public decimal PricePerDay { get; set; }\n    }\n\n    public class Customer\n    {\n        public int Id { get; set; }\n        public string Name { get; set; }\n        public string Email { get; set; }\n        public string Phone { get; set; }\n    }\n\n    public class DropOffLocation\n    {\n        public int Id { get; set; }\n        public string Address { get; set; }\n        public  public Customer Renter { get; set; }\n        public DropOffLocation DropOff { get; set; }\n        public DateTime StartDate { get; set; }\n        public DateTime EndDate { get; set; }\n        public decimal TotalPrice { get; set; }\n    }\n\n    public class RentalAgency\n    {\n        public List<Car> Cars { get; set; } = new List<Car>();\n        public List<Customer> Customers { get; set; } = new List<Customer>();\n        public List<DropOffLocation> Locations { get; set; } = new List<DropOffLocation>();\n        public List<Rental> Rentals { get; set; } = new List<Rental>();\n    }\n\n    class Program\n    {\n        static void Main(string[] args)\n        {\n            var agency = new RentalAgency();\n\n            // Popunjavanje podataka\n            agency.Cars.AddRange(new List<Car>\n            {\n                new Car { Id = 1, Model = \"Civic\", Brand = \"Honda\", Type = CarType.Sedan, IsAvailable = true, PricePerDay = 50 },\n CarType.Sedan, IsAvailable = false, PricePerDay = 100 }\n            });\n\n            agency.Customers.AddRange(new List<Customer>\n            {\n                new Customer { Id = 1, Name = \"John Doe\", Email = \"john.doe@example.com\", Phone = \"123-456-7890\" },\n                new Customer { Id = 2, Name = \"Jane Smith\", Email = \"jane.smith@example.com\", Phone = \"987-654-3210\" }\n            });\n\n            agency.Locations.AddRange(new List<DropOffLocation>\n            {\n                new DropOffLocation { Id = 1, Address = \"123 Main St\", City = \"New York\", Country = \"USA\" },\n                new DropOffLocation { Id = 2, Address = \"456 Elm St\", City = \"Los Angeles\", Country = \"USA\" }\n            });\n\n            agency.Rentals.Add(new Rental\n            {\n                Id = 1,\n                RentedCar = agency.Cars[0],\n                Renter = agency.Customers[0],\n                DropOff = agency.Locations[0],\n                StartDate = DateTime.Now.AddDays(-3),\n                EndDate = DateTime.Now,\n                TotalPrice = 150\n            });\n\n            // LINQ upiti\n            var availableCars = agency.Cars.Where(c => c.IsAvailable).ToList();\n            Console.WriteLine(\"Dostupni automobili:\");\n            foreach (var car in availableCars)\n            {\n                Console.WriteLine($\"{car.Brand} {car.Model} - {car.PricePerDay:C} po danu\");\n            }\n\n            var rentalsInNY = agency.Rentals.Where(r => r.DropOff.City == \"New York\").ToList();\n List<Vehicle> vehicles,\n        List<Booking> bookings,\n        List<SupportAgent> agents,\n        List<SupportTicket> tickets)\n    {\n        foreach (var location in locations)\n        {\n booking.Customer.Bookings.Add(booking);\n            booking.Vehicle.Bookings.Add(booking);\n        }\n\n        bookings[0].SupportTickets.Add(tickets[0]);\n {"timestamp":"2026-04-02T16:14:09.195Z","hook_event_name":"PreToolUse","session_id":"aa75618d-d4c2-47fb-9776-fe63a0f04824","transcript_path":"c:\\Users\\rober\\AppData\\Roaming\\Code\\User\\workspaceStorage\\6342806b270b2249f8fb3d4eb51eddaa\\GitHub.copilot-chat\\transcripts\\aa75618d-d4c2-47fb-9776-fe63a0f04824.jsonl","tool_name":"create_file","tool_input":{"filePath":"c:\\Users\\rober\\PrviLabos\\PrviLabos\\Services\\DropoffSupportService.cs","content":"using PrviLabos.Data;\nusing PrviLabos.Domain;\n\nnamespace PrviLabos.Services;\n\npublic class DropoffSupportService\n{\n    private readonly SupportDataContext _context;\n\n    public DropoffSupportService(SupportDataContext context)\n    {\n        _context = context;\n    }\n\n    public List<Booking> GetLateDropoffs(DateTime now)\n    {\n        return _context.Bookings\n            .Where(b => b.Status == BookingStatus.Active)\n            .Where(b => b.ActualDropoffAt is null && b.PlannedDropoffAt < now)\n            .OrderByDescending(b => b.PlannedDropoffAt)\n            .ToList();\n    }\n\n    public List<SupportTicket> GetActiveEscalations()\n    {\n        return _context.Tickets\n            .Where(t => t.Status is TicketStatus.Open or TicketStatus.InProgress or TicketStatus.Escalated)\n            .Where(t => t.Priority is TicketPriority.High or TicketPriority.Critical)\n            .OrderByDescending(t => t.Priority)\n            .ThenBy(t => t.CreatedAt)\n            .ToList();\n    }\n\n    public List<(string location, int requests)> GetMostRequestedDropoffLocations()\n    {\n        return _context.Tickets\n            .GroupBy(t => t.RequestedDropoffLocation.Name)\n            .Select(g => (location: g.Key, requests: g.Count()))\n            .OrderByDescending(x => x.requests)\n            .ThenBy(x => x.location)\n            .ToList();\n    }\n\n    public List<(string customer, decimal totalSpent)> GetTopCustomersBySpending(int top)\n    {\n        return _context.Customers\n            .Select(c => new\n            {\n                FullName = $\"{c.FirstName} {c.LastName}\",\n                TotalSpent = c.Bookings.Sum(b => b.TotalPrice)\n            })\n            .OrderByDescending(x => x.TotalSpent)\n            .Take(top)\n            .Select(x => (x.FullName, x.TotalSpent))\n            .ToList();\n    }\n\n    public async Task<int> ProcessOpenTicketsAsync()\n    {\n        var openTickets = _context.Tickets\n            .Where(t => t.Status is TicketStatus.Open or TicketStatus.InProgress or TicketStatus.Escalated)\n            .ToList();\n\n        foreach (var ticket in openTickets)\n        {\n            await SimulateExternalDropoffValidationAsync(ticket);\n            ticket.Status = TicketStatus.Resolved;\n            ticket.ResolvedAt = DateTime.UtcNow;\n\n            var booking = ticket.Booking;\n            booking.PlannedDropoffLocation = ticket.RequestedDropoffLocation;\n        }\n\n        return openTickets.Count;\n    }\n\n    private static async Task SimulateExternalDropoffValidationAsync(SupportTicket ticket)\n    {\n        var delayMs = ticket.Priority switch\n        {\n            TicketPriority.Critical => 600,\n            TicketPriority.High => 450,\n            TicketPriority.Medium => 300,\n            _ => 200\n        };\n\n        await Task.Delay(delayMs);\n    }\n}\n"},"tool_use_id":"call_gOrSWE3CYUSSWqeOxqeOw7Eo__vscode-1775145628902","cwd":"c:\\Users\\rober\\PrviLabos"}

Clarify user goals, audience, task context, and constraints.\n+2. Identify UX issues and rank them by impact and effort.\n+3. Propose a concrete UX direction with rationale tied to usability principles.\n+4. Implement focused UI/content changes when requested.\n+5. Validate that changes preserve responsiveness, accessibility basics, and user clarity.\n+\n+## Output Format\n+1. UX diagnosis\n+2. Proposed UX changes\n+3. Implementation details (if code edits are requested)\n+4. keep edits minimal and UX-targeted.\n-- DO NOT produce generic, boilerplate UI advice; provide intentional, context-aware recommendations.\n+- DO NOT produce generic, boilerplate UI advice.\n+- DO NOT ship default Bootstrap-looking layouts (standard container + plain cards + default buttons).\n+- ALWAYS deliver a distinct, intentional visual language.\n+\n+## UX Style Prompt (Primary Design Direction)\n+Use this style system by default unless the user explicitly asks for a different direction.\n+\n+### Visual Tone\n+- Professional operations dashboard with confident contrast and clear data hierarchy.\n+- Dark navigation shell + light content canvas.\n+- Crisp, information-dense blocks with generous spacing and clean separators.\n+\n+### Color System\n+- Sidebar/Navigation: deep navy `#142638`.\n+- App background: cool light gray `#F4F7FB`.\n+- Surfaces/cards: white `#FFFFFF` with subtle border `#E6ECF2`.\n+- Primary accent for charts and highlights: cyan `#27B1E6`.\n+- KPI palette:\n+  - yellow `#F0C419`\n+  - violet `#6E62C6`\n+  - green `#22B455`\n+  - sky blue `#2EA9DF`\n+- Text:\n+  - heading `#1D2A3A`\n+  - body `#4A5A6A`\n+  - muted `#7E8B98`\n+\n+### Typography\n+- Prefer modern geometric sans stacks: `\"Manrope\", \"Plus Jakarta Sans\", \"Segoe UI\", sans-serif`.\n+- Clear weight ladder: 600-700 for section titles, 500-600 for labels, 400 for body text.\n+- Tight heading line-height and slightly relaxed body line-height for scanability.\n+\n+### Component Principles\n+- Navigation: dark vertical sidebar with icon-first grouping and compact section headers.\n+- Filters/toolbar: horizontal, compact controls with strong alignment and equal heights.\n+- KPI cards: color-block cards with large numeric value + short caption; avoid default card templates.\n+- Tables: minimal borders, strong column rhythm, muted headers, high readability.\n+- Data visualization: simple line/area charts with restrained grid and one dominant accent color.\n+- Actions: icon-led actions for row-level operations; textual buttons for primary workflow actions.\n+\n+### Layout Principles\n+- Use a dashboard grid with clear zones: nav, top utilities, filters, KPIs, analytics.\n+- Prioritize progressive disclosure: summary first, drill-down second.\n+- Maintain 8px spacing rhythm (8/16/24/32 scale).\n+- Keep max content width fluid; optimize for desktop first, then stack intelligently on mobile.\n+- On mobile: collapse sidebar, keep KPIs swipe/stack friendly, preserve chart legibility.\n+\n+### Motion and Interaction\n+- Subtle, purposeful transitions only (150-250ms, ease-out).\n+- Emphasize state changes (hover, selected, filtered) without flashy animation.\n+- Use motion to reinforce hierarchy, not decoration.\n+\n+### Accessibility Baseline\n+- Ensure contrast compliance for text and key UI controls.\n+- Keep clear focus states for keyboard navigation.\n+- Provide descriptive labels and avoid color-only status indicators.\n \n ## Approach\n 1. Clarify user goals, audience, task context, and constraints.\n 2. Identify UX issues and rank them by impact and effort.\n-3. Propose a concrete UX direction with rationale tied to usability principles.\n-4. Implement focused UI/content changes when requested.\n-5. Validate that changes preserve responsiveness, accessibility basics, and user clarity.\n+3. Propose a concrete UX direction based on the UX Style Prompt above.\n+4. Define components, layout behavior, and responsive adaptations before coding.\n+5. Implement focused UI/content changes when requested.\n+6. Validate responsiveness, accessibility basics, clarity, and visual uniqueness.\n \n ## Output Format\n-1. UX diagnosis\n-2. Proposed UX changes\n-3. Implementation details (if code edits are requested)\n-4. Validation checklist and residual UX risks\n+1. UX diagnosis (top issues by impact)\n+2. Visual direction (palette, typography, mood)\n+3. Component and layout spec\n+4. Implementation details (if code edits are requested)\n+5. Hub\", \"Home\", \"Index\"),\n+            new(\"Tickets\")\n+        };\n+\n+        var tickets = _repositories.Tickets\n+            .GetAll()\n+            .OrderByDescending(t => t.Priority)\n+ new(ticket.TicketNumber)\n+        };\n+\n+        return View(ticket);\n+    }\n+\n+    public static string GetPriorityClass(TicketPriority priority)\n+    {\n+        return priority switch\n+        {\n+            TicketPriority.Critical => \"is-critical\",\n+            TicketPriority.High => \"is-high\",\n+            TicketPriority.Medium => \"is-medium\",\n+            _ => \"is-low\"\n+    TicketStatus.InProgress => \"is-medium\",\n+            TicketStatus.Open => \"is-high\",\n+            TicketStatus.Resolved => \"is-low\",\n+            _ => \"is-low\"\n+        };\n+
